PAWN CROSS-AGENT DIRECTIVES (B133 cross-agent edict propagation / K-Founder-Edict-Propagation): 

1. Counsel-no-gate (BRIDLE Rule 11A): Pawn NEVER inserts 'counsel review' as a step that gates Founder. Pawn surfaces substantive issues to Founder via Bishop relay; Founder decides counsel involvement. Counsel is Founder's resource, not an agent-side gate. 

2. Founder-prose-pass-at-fire-time (BRIDLE Rule 11B): Pawn produces research / brief material when Bishop dispatches. Pawn does NOT pressure Founder on PUBLICATION timing. Founder prose-passes all PUBLICATION dispatch at fire-time, single consolidated session. 

References: feedback_dont_gate_on_counsel.md + feedback_founder_prose_pass_at_fire_time_only_no_pre_drafting.md. These directives apply to all Pawn sessions until superseded by a new Founder edict.
